title: C# Basic — Paper 6
description: C# concepts: conditionals, switch expressions, loops, loop control, arrays, constructors, access modifiers, properties, static members, and inheritance basics.

questions.text: What is a ternary operator (`? :`) in C# equivalent to?

options.text: A switch expression
An if-else statement that returns a value
A null-coalescing expression
A loop that iterates twice

questions.text: What is the output of `Console.WriteLine(x > 0 ? "pos" : "non-pos")` when `x = -3`?

options.text: pos
non-pos
Compile error

questions.text: In C#, can a `switch` statement match on enum values?

options.text: No, switch only works with integers and strings
Yes, enum values are valid switch expressions in C#
Only if the enum has the [Flags] attribute
Only if the enum is cast to int first

questions.text: What does a `switch` expression with property pattern `obj is { Name: "Alice" }` check?

options.text: Whether obj has a field called Name
Whether the Name property of obj equals "Alice"
Whether obj is null
Whether obj's type is Alice

questions.text: Which pattern combines multiple conditions in a switch expression arm in C#?

options.text: or, and, not patterns (e.g., `< 0 or > 100`)
|| and && operators inside the arm
Multiple `when` clauses
C# does not support combined patterns

questions.text: What is an infinite `while` loop in C#?

options.text: while (1) { }
while (true) { }
while (false) { }
while { }

questions.text: What is the correct syntax for a nested for loop to print a 3x3 grid in C#?

options.text: for (int i=0;i<3;i++) for (int j=0;j<3;j++) Console.Write($"{i},{j} ");
for (int i,j=0;i<3 && j<3;i++,j++) Console.Write(i);
foreach (int i in 3) foreach (int j in 3) Console.Write(i);
for 3 loop { for 3 loop { print; } }

questions.text: What does `break` do inside a `while` loop when a condition is met midway?

options.text: Skips to the next iteration
Restarts the loop
Exits the while loop immediately
Throws a BreakException

questions.text: What is the effect of `continue` in a `for` loop in C#?

options.text: Exits the loop
Skips remaining statements in the body and proceeds to the iterator expression
Moves to the outer loop if nested
Re-evaluates the initialization

questions.text: How do you declare a `goto` label and jump to it in C#?

options.text: label: and jump label;
@label: and go label;
myLabel: and goto myLabel;
#label and goto #label;

questions.text: What does `return` do inside a `foreach` loop that is inside a method?

options.text: Exits only the foreach loop
Exits the entire method
Continues to the next iteration
Throws an InvalidOperationException

questions.text: How do you declare a one-dimensional array of 5 integers in C#?

options.text: int[] arr = new int[5];
int arr[5];
array int arr = 5;
int arr = new int{5};

questions.text: How do you declare a 2D array of 3 rows and 4 columns in C#?

options.text: int[][] arr = new int[3][4];
int[,] arr = new int[3, 4];
int arr[3,4] = new int;
int[3][4] arr;

questions.text: What is a jagged array in C#?

options.text: A 2D array with all rows having the same length
An array where each row is an independent array that can have different lengths
An array of nullable elements
An array that supports only strings

questions.text: What does `Array.Sort(arr)` do in C#?

options.text: Returns a new sorted copy of the array
Sorts the array in descending order
Sorts the array in ascending order in place
Throws NotSupportedException for integer arrays

questions.text: What does `Array.Copy(source, dest, length)` do in C#?

options.text: Creates a new array that is a copy of source
Copies `length` elements from source into dest starting at index 0
Copies dest into source
Merges source and dest into a new array

questions.text: What is the purpose of a default constructor in C#?

options.text: It sets all fields to their maximum values
It is a parameterless constructor that initializes an object to a default state
It is called automatically to destroy an object
It cannot be overridden

questions.text: In constructor chaining, what is `:base(args)` used for in C#?

options.text: Calling another constructor in the same class
Calling the parent class constructor from a derived class constructor
Accessing the base class's static members
Creating a copy of the base class

questions.text: Can a static constructor have access modifiers in C#?

options.text: Yes, it is typically public
Yes, it can be public or private
No, static constructors cannot have access modifiers
Yes, it must be protected

questions.text: What does the `internal` modifier ensure in a C# multi-project solution?

options.text: The member is accessible to all projects
The member is accessible only within the assembly (project) it is declared in
The member is accessible only within the class
The member is accessible to derived classes only

questions.text: What does `private protected` restrict access to compared to `protected` alone in C#?

options.text: `private protected` is more restrictive: accessible only within the same assembly AND by derived classes in the same assembly
`private protected` is less restrictive: accessible by any derived class
They are identical
`private protected` allows access from the same file only

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `value` keyword in a property setter in C#?

options.text: It refers to the existing value of the property
It represents the value being assigned to the property
It is a built-in constant equal to 0
It marks the field as immutable

questions.text: What does `public string Name { get; private set; }` allow?

options.text: Reading from anywhere; writing only from within the class
Reading and writing from anywhere
Reading only from within the class; writing from anywhere
Writing only; no reading allowed

questions.text: Can a property's `get` accessor be more restrictive than its `set` accessor in C#?

options.text: Yes, always
Yes, but only if both are explicitly declared
No, C# requires the `set` to be at least as restrictive as the `get`
No, and this causes a compile-time error

questions.text: What is the static constructor syntax in C#?

options.text: public static MyClass() { }
static MyClass() { }
MyClass.static() { }
private static MyClass() { }

questions.text: What is `Math.PI` in C# an example of?

options.text: An instance field
A const in a static class
A static method
An extension method

questions.text: Can a static class in C# have instance constructors?

options.text: Yes, one instance constructor is allowed
Yes, unlimited instance constructors
No, static classes cannot have instance constructors
Only protected instance constructors are allowed

questions.text: What does the `virtual` keyword allow in a C# base class method?

options.text: It prevents the method from being overridden
It allows derived classes to override the method with their own implementation
It makes the method static
It hides the method from derived classes

questions.text: What does the `override` keyword do in a derived class in C#?

options.text: Creates a new version of a method, hiding the base class version
Provides a new implementation for a `virtual` or `abstract` method from the base class
Makes the method inaccessible from outside the class
Prevents further overriding in derived classes

questions.text: What is an interface in C#?

options.text: A class that cannot be instantiated
A contract defining a set of members that implementing classes must provide
An abstract class with only one abstract method
A sealed class that prevents inheritance
